example.en: I need to cut my hair.

exampleSentences: Chị muốn cắt tóc ngắn hơn vào mùa hè này.
Em thấy tóc của anh hôm nay trông rất đẹp.
Mỗi sáng mẹ đều giúp bé chải tóc trước khi đi học.
notes:
tóc means hair (the hair on your head). It’s an uncountable noun in general, but you can count strands with sợi tóc (a strand of hair).

Common pairings: cắt tóc (cut hair), nhuộm tóc (dye hair), uốn tóc (curl hair), chải tóc (comb hair).  
To describe it, add adjectives after: tóc dài/ngắn, tóc đen/vàng, tóc thẳng/xoăn.

You’ll also hear mái tóc for a whole head of hair (a bit more expressive), and tóc tai when talking about someone’s hair in a general or slightly messy way.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I want to cut my hair shorter this summer.
I think your hair looks very nice today.
Every morning, mom helps the child brush their hair before going to school.
notes_md:
**tóc** means **hair** (the hair on your head). It’s an uncountable noun in general, but you can count strands with **sợi tóc** (a strand of hair).

Common pairings: **cắt tóc** (cut hair), **nhuộm tóc** (dye hair), **uốn tóc** (curl hair), **chải tóc** (comb hair).  
To describe it, add adjectives after: **tóc dài/ngắn**, **tóc đen/vàng**, **tóc thẳng/xoăn**.

You’ll also hear **mái tóc** for a whole head of hair (a bit more expressive), and **tóc tai** when talking about someone’s hair in a general or slightly messy way.
